description: Fix the provider panel in the Kanban UI: backend returns nested data that doesn't match frontend ProviderStatus type, causing providers to never display. Add flattened pool status endpoint, transform layer in frontend, and enhanced UI with soft/hard toggle support.

userStories.title: Flatten backend pool status response to match frontend ProviderStatus type
userStories.description:
As a UI developer, I want the /pool/status endpoint to return a flat array of provider objects matching the frontend ProviderStatus interface, so the UI can display them without transformation.

The bug: get_pool_status() returns {providers: [...], usage: {...}} with usage stats in a separate dict. The frontend fetchPoolStatus() expects ProviderStatus[] directly but gets the nested object. Fields like health, rpm, max_rpm, circuit_state are missing or misplaced.

Fix: Enrich get_pool_status() to flatten usage stats into each provider dict, compute health from circuit state, compute RPM from request_timestamps, include max_rpm from config, and flatten circuit_state.
acceptanceCriteria: get_pool_status() returns providers array where each provider includes: name, type, priority, enabled, health (derived from circuit state), rpm (60s window), max_rpm (from config), circuit_state (string), total_cost_usd, avg_latency_ms, model (from config)
health is derived: closed->healthy, half_open->degraded, open->unhealthy
rpm is computed from request_timestamps in the last 60 seconds
Existing tests still pass (uv run pytest tests/ -q)
No regressions in existing pool toggle tests

userStories.title: Fix frontend fetchPoolStatus to handle backend response structure
userStories.description:
As a user viewing the Kanban UI, I want the provider list to actually populate from the backend data.

The bug: fetchPoolStatus() calls GET /api/pool/status and expects ProviderStatus[] but the endpoint returns {providers: [...], active: bool, usage: {...}, strategy: string}. The frontend needs to extract the providers array.

Fix: Update fetchPoolStatus() in api.ts to extract the providers array from the response wrapper. Also update the usePoolStatus hook and WebSocket handler if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchPoolStatus() extracts providers array from the {providers, active, ...} wrapper
usePoolStatus React Query hook returns ProviderStatus[] correctly
WebSocket pool_update handler correctly updates the React Query cache

userStories.title: Redesign ProviderPoolStatus UI with expandable rows and soft/hard toggle
userStories.description:
As a user managing providers, I want to see each provider with its health, stats, and model info, with clear soft (runtime) and hard (persisted) toggle controls.

Fix: Redesign ProviderPoolStatus to show:
- Provider name + type badge
- Health indicator (dot + label)
- RPM gauge, cost, latency
- Toggle switch for runtime enable/disable (soft)
- Save button that persists to config (hard)
- Model name if available
acceptanceCriteria: Each provider row shows: toggle, name, type badge, health dot+label, RPM, cost, latency, model
Toggle is clearly labeled as runtime-only (soft toggle)
Save/Persist button is clearly labeled as permanent (hard toggle)
Component works with empty provider list (shows placeholder)
